title: Generative AI Digital Twins for Asthma
excerpt: Excited to have presented our work on generative AI-powered digital twins for the Cure Asthma initiative at the TSANZSRS Annual Scientific Meeting 2026 in Perth. Asthma remains a complex and heterogeneous disease, where many patients continue to experience exacerbations despite standard care. Our work aims to move beyond reactive management towards predictive and personalised medicine by leveraging large-scale clinical and multi-modal data. By developing digital twins powered by generative AI, we can simulate disease trajectories, anticipate exacerbations, and explore personalised treatment strategies in silico. This opens up new opportunities to better understand remission pathways and design more targeted interventions. A big thank you to the organisers and the broader respiratory research community for the inspiring discussions. Great to see such strong momentum towards data-driven, patient-centred approaches in respiratory medicine. Looking forward to continued collaborations across clinical, computational, and patient communities to drive this forward.
